archetype: High-Level Radioactive Waste Transport Real-Time Integrity and Chain-of-Custody Orchestration System
description: A safety-critical system that monitors, authenticates, and logs all parameters of legal high-level radioactive waste (HLW) transport casks during multi-jurisdictional transit, ensuring continuous physical integrity, radiation containment, and unbroken regulatory chain-of-custody from repository/shutdown site to interim storage or processing facility. It integrates with cask-mounted sensor suites, GPS/INS navigation, and government radiation portal monitors, providing cryptographic audit trails for IAEA and national regulators like the NRC and Euratom.
rules: MUST enforce cryptographic signing of all sensor data at the source device per IAEA INFCIRC/225/Rev.5 (Physical Protection of Nuclear Material) and SSR-6 (Regulations for the Safe Transport of Radioactive Material).
MUST implement geofencing with automatic regulatory authority alerting if a transport deviates from an pre-approved, licensed route or schedule.

MUST maintain a dual-write, immutable audit log—one stream locally on the transport vehicle's Hardware Security Module (HSM) and one stream to a regulator-owned cloud endpoint—with no single point of failure.
MUST authenticate and validate data from third-party radiation portal monitors and weigh-in-motion scales at jurisdictional checkpoints using mutually authenticated TLS and pre-shared public keys.

MUST provide a 'tamper-evident' operator interface that locks all controls and initiates a silent security protocol if unauthorized physical or network access is detected, per national nuclear transport security regulations.
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely on standard commercial GPS alone; it must be fused with inertial navigation and dead reckoning to detect and log GPS spoofing/jamming, a known threat in nuclear transport.

DO NOT use a generic blockchain for audit trails; the deterministic, append-only log must be formally verified and compatible with legacy regulator systems that require specific, non-repudiable digital signature standards (e.g., FIPS 186-4).

DO NOT design for eventual consistency; all critical integrity checks (cask seal status, radiation levels, door locks) must be synchronously validated with the central system or fall back to a safe, communications-blackout mode with local quorum-based decision making.
key_technical_challenges: Achieving sub-second, reliable telemetry from moving vehicles across heterogeneous, often low-bandwidth networks (cellular, satellite, private radio) with guaranteed message ordering and no data loss during coverage gaps.
Designing a hardened, real-time embedded agent on the cask that can perform cryptographic operations, sensor fusion, and fail-safe decision logic on resource-constrained, radiation-hardened hardware.

Integrating with a fragmented global regulatory landscape where each country/state has different data format requirements (e.g., US NRC Form 741 vs. EU's SURE system) for manifest and event reporting, requiring dynamic, configurable data transformation pipelines.

recommended_stack.language: Rust for embedded cask agent (memory safety, no GC, direct hardware access) and Go for central orchestration services (concurrency, network services).
recommended_stack.framework: Actix/axum for Rust embedded HTTP server; Gin for Go orchestration APIs. Custom FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated crypto module using OpenSSL or dedicated HSM SDKs.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB for high-frequency time-series sensor data; PostgreSQL with pgcrypto for relational audit logs and manifest data. Immutable append-only tables with BRIN indexing for regulatory queries.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis with ACLs and TLS for transient state and recent checkpoint data, with explicit discard on security event.
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Pulsar with geo-replication and tiered storage to handle guaranteed delivery across disconnected networks, using its built-in support for delayed messages for scheduled checkpoint alerts.
special_services: Custom hardware abstraction layer (HAL) for interfacing with radiation sensors (e.g., Ludlum Model 44-9), seal switches, and door interlocks via CAN bus/RS-485.
Regulatory Reporting Transformation Service: A rules engine (Drools) that dynamically maps internal event schemas to jurisdiction-specific XML/EDIFACT manifest formats for automated filings.
Adversary Emulation & Simulation Service: A digital twin of the transport that injects sensor faults, network partitions, and physical tamper scenarios to test system resilience offline.

compliance_flags: US NRC 10 CFR Part 71 (Packaging and Transport of Radioactive Material)
EU ADR (European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road)
National nuclear transport security directives (e.g., US DOE Order 460.2D, UK Nuclear Industries Security Regulations)
